title: taraxacum sect. taraxacum
intro: Taraxacum sect. taraxacum, also known as the common dandelion, is a plant species within the Taraxacum genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and jagged, tooth-like leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of bioactive compounds, including taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum sect. taraxacum have been linked to various health benefits, including the reduction of blood pressure, improvement of digestion, and support of liver function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be consumed as tea, capsules, or tinctures, often used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to gastrointestinal issues.
